title: zehneria rutenbergiana
intro: Zehneria rutenbergiana, also known as the East African wild cucumber, is a perennial vine native to tropical Africa. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The East African wild cucumber is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, and is often used to treat conditions like arthritis and bronchitis. In herbal preparations, Zehneria rutenbergiana is often used in decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
